communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Any policies relating to the usage of Large language models, and other "AI".

Retention policies of AI/LLM chat logs and image creations.

From January 1 2022 to the present: 

1) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Chicago Transit Authority employees and any contracted workers and the following domains:

@openai.com
@chatgpt.com
@anthropic.com
@claude.ai
@gab.ai
@jasper.ai

2) A log of emails sent and/or received between current or former Chicago Transit Authority employees and any contracted workers and the following addresses:
m365copilotupdates@microsoft.com
microsoft365copilot@communication.microsoft.com
google-gemini-noreply@google.com

To make things simpler, this request does not include the contents of the emails. The information I am hoping for is: Recipient, sender, date received/sent, and subject line.

I believe this should be exportable via Google Vault or Microsoft Purview.

If your agency has a retention policy for this data shorter than this I would be grateful if you informed me so I can adjust my request time frame.

I would prefer the data in text searchable formats, such as CSV or Excel, if possible.

If you have any questions or wish to clarify any part of this request feel free to let me know. If this request is too wide, feel free to contact me to explore the possibility of adjusting the scope of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Olivia

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I hereby request the following public records related to the faculty recruitment and hiring process for the 2026-2027 (current) academic cycle within the Grainger College of Engineering.

This request includes records for tenure-track, non-tenure track, teaching, and Specialized Faculty positions. The specific list of job openings/requisition ID include, but are not limited to:
1. Department of Aerospace Engineering - Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032680 Posted on 10/8/2025
2. Department of Industrial & Enterprise Systems Engineering- Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1032898 Posted on 10/13/2025
3. Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering – Open Rank Faculty Search / Requisition ID 1033201 Posted on 11/11/2025
4. Department of Bioengineering - Teaching Faculty (Open Rank) / Requisition ID 1034104 Posted on 12/19/2025
5. The Grainger College of Engineering: Professor - Siebel School of Computing and Data Science / Requisition ID 1035082 Posted on 3/20/2026

Requested Records:

Please note that the request is for any and all interviews which may have occurred between January 1, 2026 and April 25, 2026.

1. Interview Recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of candidate screening interviews, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026).

2. Faculty Candidate Seminar recordings: Copies of any and all video or audio recordings of faculty candidate seminars, specifically for candidates who provided written consent for such recordings to be made and maintained, as outlined in the UIUC Recruitment and Hiring Guidelines (Effective January 1, 2026), even if the Faculty Candidate Seminar is publically advertised as a Departmental Seminar due to confidentiality requests from the candidate.

3. Interview Notes: Copies of all formal interview notes taken by search committee members (excluding personal "memory aides" not shared with the committee, as defined by Illinois law).

This request specifically EXCLUDES:

1. Evaluation Documents: Search committee evaluation forms, rubrics, ranking sheets, and final reports regarding the candidates for the specified positions.
2. Correspondence: Emails, memoranda, and internal communications regarding the selection, ranking, and final recommendations for these hires.
3. Meeting minutes of the Faculty Hiring Committee.


Privacy and Redactions:

Consistent with Section 7(1)(b) and 7(1)(c) of the Act, I request that you redact sensitive personal information such as Social Security Numbers (SSN), home addresses, personal phone numbers, and any other "unique identifiers" as defined by FOIA.

If you determine that any specific record or portion of a record is exempt from disclosure (e.g., the names of unsuccessful candidates who did not consent), please provide a written justification for each redaction or denial, citing the specific FOIA exemption, and release the remaining non-exempt portions of the records.

Format of Records:
I request that these records be provided in their original electronic format (e.g., PDF, MP4, or Excel) via email or a secure file-sharing link.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within the five (5) business days required by the Act.

Sincerely,

Joseph Sentien Sief

title: Sunshine Law Request: Flock audits (Casselberry Police Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sunshine Law Request: Flock audits (Casselberry Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sunshine Law Request: Flock audits (Casselberry Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good morning,
This email is to confirm that we have received your public records request. Your request is currently under review, and we will begin processing it in accordance with applicable laws and department policies. If additional information or clarification is needed to fulfill your request, we will contact you directly.
Thank you for reaching out to the Casselberry Police Department. If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us.

 Please note that we have recently experienced an increase in public records requests. We process all requests in the order they are received. As a result, there may be a delay in providing the requested documents.

Thank you,
Casselberry Police Department Records Unit
115 Wilshire Blvd
Casselberry, FL 32707
(407) 262-7616 Ext. 2007
[cid:image001.png@01DCD0A9.776DD790]

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sunshine Law Request: Flock audits (Casselberry Police Department)
communications.communication:

Your request has been completed.

Please see attached documents, provided by our FLOCK Administrator.

Thank you,
